ocr.text: The ACR classification criteria encompass various clinical manifestations relevant to patient diagnosis, with corresponding
patient numbers and percentages reflecting their prevalence. For the criterion of Malar rash, 46 patients are affected, accounting
for 67%. The Discoid rash is present in 18 patients, representing 26%. Photosensitivity affects 42 patients, which is 61% of the
cohort. Oral ulcers are reported in 16 patients, corresponding to 23%. Arthritis is the most prevalent criterion, with 55 patients
affected, making up 80% of the group. Serositis and Nephritis both have 35 patients each, indicating a prevalence of 51% for
both criteria. Neurological disorder is significantly less common, affecting only 3 patients, which is 4%. Hematological disorder
impacts 38 patients, representing 55%, while Immunological disorder is noted in 53 patients, reflecting a prevalence of 77%.
Lastly, all patients in the study exhibit Anti-nuclear antibodies, totaling 69, which constitutes 100% of the cohort. The data
reveals vital insights into the distribution of these criteria among patients, underscoring the varying severity and frequency of
symptoms in the context of diagnosis and management of related conditions. Overall, the comprehensive enumeration of these
criteria enhances our understanding of the clinical landscape associated with the ACR classification.
